text: In this life, you don't have to prove nothin' to nobody but yourself. And after what you've gone through, if you haven't done that by now, it ain't gonna never happen.
context: The groundskeeper Fortune delivers the ultimate message of self-worth and proving things only to yourself.

text: You're five feet nothing, a hundred and nothing, and you have hardly a speck of athletic ability. And you hung in there with the best college football players in the land for two years.
context: Highlighting that sheer persistence and heart matter more than physical gifts or size.

text: Having dreams is what makes life tolerable.
context: Rudy's best friend Pete reminds him that dreams are essential to enduring a hard life.

text: It's not the size of the dog in the fight, it's the size of the fight in the dog.
context: The classic underdog philosophy that defines Rudy's entire journey.

text: You're gonna walk outta here with a degree from the University of Notre Dame. In this life, you don't have to prove nothin' to nobody but yourself.
context: The full powerful reminder of what Rudy has already achieved simply by refusing to quit.

text: Someday, you're gonna look back on this and you're gonna be proud of yourself.
context: Encouragement that the struggle itself builds the pride, regardless of the final scoreboard.

text: I came here to play football for the Irish. And I'm gonna finish what I started.
context: Rudy's unwavering commitment even after years of rejection and hardship.

text: When people say dreams don't come true, tell them about Rudy.
context: The enduring legacy of a dreamer who simply would not quit.
